title: Init auto-runs bootstrap + config-install + hooks (integrated one-shot flow)
description: After core initFabric() completes, orchestrate three additional stages inside the init command handler: (1) bootstrap stage — invoke installBootstrap() for detected clients unless --no-bootstrap; (2) MCP config stage — invoke resolveClients() + each writer.write() unless --no-mcp; (3) hooks stage — invoke the hooks installer unless --no-hooks. Each stage prints a section header and respects --force for overwrite decisions. Final summary prints stage outcomes. 'fabric init --no-bootstrap --no-mcp --no-hooks' must behave identically to today's pure init.

files.change: After the core initFabric() call in the run() handler, add three conditional orchestration blocks for bootstrap, MCP config, and hooks stages; import installBootstrap from bootstrap.ts, resolveClients from config/resolver.ts, resolveServerPath from config.ts, and the hooks installer logic from hooks.ts.
Ensure installBootstrap() is exported as a standalone async function (not only wired to the citty command) so init.ts can import and call it directly.
Extract the core hook installation logic into an exported installHooks(target: string, options?) async function so init.ts can call it directly without going through citty.
Add cli.init.stages.bootstrap, cli.init.stages.mcp, cli.init.stages.hooks section header keys and cli.init.stages.skipped / cli.init.stages.completed status keys.
Mirror all stage header and status keys added to en.ts.

implementation: Read packages/cli/src/commands/bootstrap.ts to confirm installBootstrap() is exported or wrap the install logic in a new exported function; if currently only accessible through citty command, extract the inner logic.
Read packages/cli/src/commands/hooks.ts to confirm the husky install logic; extract to an exported installHooks(target, options?) function that mirrors what hooksCommand.run() does.

In init.ts run() handler, after the await initFabric() call, add a bootstrap stage block: if (!args['no-bootstrap']) { print section header; detect clients with resolveClients(); call installBootstrap() for detected bootstrap clients; print result }.

Add MCP config stage block: if (!args['no-mcp']) { print section header; resolveClients(); resolveServerPath(); call writer.write(serverPath, target) for each writer; print result — pass force from args }.
Add hooks stage block: if (!args['no-hooks']) { print section header; call installHooks(target, { force: args.force }); print result }.
Add cli.init.stages.* i18n keys to both locale files.
Verify that 'fabric init --no-bootstrap --no-mcp --no-hooks' produces identical output to the current 'fab init' — write a brief snapshot test or add a manual check step.
Run pnpm test to confirm no regressions.

reference.pattern: Extract inner logic into standalone exported functions, import in init.ts, call conditionally based on options flags; same pattern used by resolveClients() which is already shared between config.ts and bootstrap.ts
reference.examples: exploration-architecture.json (installBootstrap, L151-L165): already overwrite-friendly; exploration-integration-points.json (GAP-2): init must call resolveClients() to auto-configure MCP clients

anchor_context: packages/cli/src/commands/hooks.ts:L36-L96 — hooks install logic + FAB_BIN= skip check
exploration-integration-points.json (init_artifact_catalog) — what init creates vs what config install creates
exploration-integration-points.json (GAP-2) — gap: init does not call config install

criteria: 'fabric init' on a fresh project: creates core artifacts + bootstrap files (for detected clients) + MCP config entries + husky hook in a single pass
'fabric init --no-bootstrap --no-mcp --no-hooks' produces the same output as today's 'fab init' (only core AGENTS.md + .fabric/ + .claude/ scaffold)
Each integrated stage prints a distinct section header so user can track progress
'fabric init --force' propagates force flag to all three sub-stages
No regression on 'fabric bootstrap install' or 'fabric config install' called standalone

rationale.chosen_approach: Orchestrate by importing and calling exported functions from sibling command modules; each stage is conditional on skipX flags
alternatives_considered: Spawn child processes for each sub-command — too heavy and loses in-process error handling
Create a new 'fabric setup' meta-command — adds surface area; user said 'init is the sole entrypoint'
decision_factors: User said: 'init is the SOLE user-facing entrypoint; other commands should be hidden'
opt-out flags (--no-bootstrap, --no-mcp, --no-hooks) preserve escape hatch for users who want partial init
In-process function calls keep error handling and logging consistent
rationale.tradeoffs: init.ts becomes larger; mitigated by delegating each stage to its own module function

manual_checks: fabric init on fresh Claude-only project: AGENTS.md + .fabric/ + .claude/ hooks + CLAUDE.md bootstrap + .claude/settings.json mcpServers entry + .husky/pre-commit all created
fabric init --no-bootstrap --no-mcp --no-hooks: only AGENTS.md + .fabric/ + .claude/ hooks created — identical to current behavior
fabric init --force on initialized project: all stages forced
success_metrics: Manual one-shot init on clean fixture creates all expected artifacts without additional commands
integration: init-integrated: one-shot creates all artifact layers
